title: Beware of Phishing Attacks via Fake Bank SMS and Calls
excerpt: CERT-In has observed a surge in phishing campaigns where fraudsters impersonate Indian banks via SMS and voice calls, directing victims to fake websites or extracting OTPs and passwords over phone. Citizens should verify all communications through official bank portals and never share credentials over unverified channels.

title: Advisory on Vishing (Voice Phishing) Attacks
excerpt: Vishing attacks use automated voice calls or live callers pretending to be bank officials, government agents, or courier companies. Attackers induce panic by claiming legal action, account suspension, or parcel seizure and then request OTPs, card details, or fund transfers. Hang up and call back using numbers from the official website.

title: Remote Access Trojan Delivered via Fake Tech Support Calls
excerpt: CERT-In has issued guidance on tech-support scams where callers claim your device is infected and instruct you to install remote-access software (AnyDesk, QuickSupport, TeamViewer). Once installed, attackers gain full control and execute banking fraud. No legitimate organisation proactively calls to fix device issues.

title: Digital Arrest Fraud: Fake CBI / Police Video Calls
excerpt: CERT-In has issued a warning about 'Digital Arrest' scams where fraudsters conduct fake video calls posing as CBI, ED, or Police officers and claim the victim is under digital surveillance for money laundering or drug trafficking. They demand immediate payments to 'cancel the arrest'. No law enforcement agency in India conducts arrests or demands payment via video call.

title: Investment Fraud via Social Media and Messaging Apps
excerpt: CERT-In warns of investment scams propagated through WhatsApp groups, Telegram channels, and Instagram ads promising guaranteed high returns on stocks, crypto, or forex trading. Victims are added to fake VIP groups, shown fabricated profits, and then asked to invest larger sums before withdrawals are blocked. Report to cybercrime.gov.in and call 1930.
